<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Cohesion of 1.0 across 22 test files is a strong signal that the e2e suite has clear, consistent scope — no stray production or configuration files have drifted into this zone.
[observation] [info] architecture-policy.test.js enforces spawn-only, intra-package layering, and zone-cycle constraints — these tests are critical path for the four-tier dependency hierarchy and should never be skipped in CI.
[observation] [info] mcp-transport.test.js validates HTTP session management end-to-end; as the server gains new MCP tools, corresponding transport-level test cases should be added here to maintain coverage parity.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling indicates a tightly self-contained test suite — all 22 files are part of the same test community and share no runtime imports with production code.
- The presence of architecture-policy.test.js and domain-isolation.test.js within the e2e suite means structural violations are caught at the process boundary level, complementing the unit-level gateway tests in mcp-route-layer.
- Test file names map one-to-one with orchestration commands (cli-ci, cli-config, cli-delegation, cli-dev) — this naming discipline makes it easy to identify which test covers which command when debugging regressions.
- Cohesion of 1.0 across 22 test files is a strong signal that the e2e suite has clear, consistent scope — no stray production or configuration files have drifted into this zone.
- architecture-policy.test.js enforces spawn-only, intra-package layering, and zone-cycle constraints — these tests are critical path for the four-tier dependency hierarchy and should never be skipped in CI.
- mcp-transport.test.js validates HTTP session management end-to-end; as the server gains new MCP tools, corresponding transport-level test cases should be added here to maintain coverage parity.
- [call graph] 453 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)
